title: DAC 2026 See Analog Bits TSMC N2P IP portfolio and meet with its engineering experts! - Semiwiki
gist: Thin DAC 2026 booth promo for Analog Bits, a mixed-signal IP vendor with over 1,000 IP products across 75+ process nodes and all five major foundries (TSMC, Samsung, Intel, GlobalFoundries, RAPIDUS, UMC), used by 400+ customers including hyperscaler AI accelerator programs, though no specific customers or financial figures are disclosed.

themes.theme: Analog Bits' scale and foundry-agnostic IP portfolio

insights.claim: Analog Bits has over 1,000 IP products qualified on 75+ process nodes and used by over 400 customers globally, including deployment in top global hyperscalers' AI accelerator programs, leading North American automotive OEMs' next-generation SoC programs, and major US big-tech custom silicon data-center programs, though specific customer names are withheld due to NDA practices.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Scale figures (1,000+ IP products, 400+ customers, all five major foundries) establish Analog Bits as a broad-based mixed-signal IP supplier to the AI accelerator supply chain, though the lack of named customers limits verifiability of specific claims.
insights.quote: Analog Bits has over 1,000+ IP products on 75+ process nodes and used by over 400 customers globally.

insights.claim: Analog Bits' core differentiator is process portability across nodes and foundries, a first-time silicon success track record, pre-integrated and customizable IP for SoC platforms, and extremely low-power designs validated in silicon, and the company is showcasing its TSMC N2P (2nm) IP portfolio at DAC 2026.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Confirms Analog Bits already has qualified IP for TSMC's N2P (2nm-class) node, positioning it as an early mixed-signal IP option for chip designers moving to TSMC's most advanced nodes.
insights.quote: You can visit Analog Bits at DAC 2026 at booth number 949 to see live demonstrations of the company's TSMC N2P IP portfolio
